author: kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
verses:
nadīyā⋅udayagiri, pūrṇa-candra gaura⋅hari,
kṛpā kari’ hoila udaya
pāpa⋅tamo haila nāśa, trijagatera ullāsa,
jagabhari’ hari-dhvani haya

seikāle nijālaya, uṭhiyā advaita rāya,
nṛtya kare ānandita⋅mane
hari-dāse lañā saṅge, huṅkāra⋅kīrtana⋅raṅge,
kene nāce keha nāhi jāne

dekhi’ uparāga hāsi, śīghra gaṅgā-ghāṭe āsi’,
ānande karila gaṅgāsnāna
pāñā uparāga⋅chale, āpanāra manobale,
brāhmaṇere dila nānā dāna

jagat ānandamaya, dekhi’ mane savismaya,
ṭhāre⋅ṭhore kahe haridāsa
‘tomāra aichana raṅga, mora mana parasanna,
dekhi⋅kichu kārye āche bhāsa’

ācārya⋅ratna, śrīvāsa, haila mane sukhollāsa,
jāi’ snāna kaila gaṅgājale
ānande vihvala mana, kare hari saŋkīrtana,
nānā dāna kaila manobale

ei mata bhakta⋅yati, jā̐’ra jei deśe sthiti,
tāhā̐ tāhā̐ pāñā manobale
nāce kare saṅkīrtana, ānande vihvala mana,
dāna kare grahaṇera chale

brāhmaṇa⋅sajjana⋅nārī, nānā dravye thāli bhari’,
āilā sabe jautuka loiyā
jena kā̐cāsonā⋅dyuti, dekhi’ bālakera mūrti,
āśīrvāda kare sukha pāñā

sāvitrī, gaurī, sarasvatī, śacī, rambhā, arundhatī,
āra jata deva⋅nārīgaṇa
nānā dravye pātra bhari’, brāhmaṇīra veśa dhari’,
āsi’ sabe karena daraśana

antarīkṣe devagaṇa, gandharva, siddha, cāraṇa,
stuti⋅nṛtya kare vādya⋅gīta
nartaka, vādaka, bhāṭa, navadvīpe jā’ra nāṭa,
sabe āsi’ nāce pāñā prīta

kebā āse kebā jāya, kebā nāce kebā gāya,
sambhālite nāre kāra bola
khaṇḍileka duḥkha-śoka, pramoda-pūrita loka,
miśra hailā ānande vihvala

ācāryaratna, śrīvāsa, jagannāthamiśra⋅pāśa,
āsi’ tā̐re kare sāvadhāna
karāila jāta-karma, je āchila vidhi⋅dharma,
tabe miśra kare nānā dāna

jautuka pāila jata, ghare vā āchila kata,
saba dhana vipre dila dāna
jata nartaka, gāyana, bhāṭa, akiñcana jana,
dhana diyā kailā sabāra māna

śrīvāsera brāhmaṇī, nāma tā̐’ra ‘mālinī’,
ācāryaratnera patnī⋅saṅge
sindura, haridrā, taila, khoi, kalā, nānā phala,
diyā pūje nārīgaṇa raṅge

advaita ācārya⋅bhāryā, jagat⋅pūjitā āryā,
nāma tā̐’ra ‘sītā ṭhākurāṇī’
ācāryera ājñā pāñā, gelā upahāra lañā,
dekhite bālaka⋅śiromaṇi

suvarṇera kaḍi⋅bouli, rajatamudrā⋅pāśuli,
suvarṇera aṅgada, kaṅkaṇa
du⋅bāhute divya śaṅkha, rajatera malabaṅka,
svarṇamudrāra nānā hāra-gaṇa

vyāghra-nakha hemajaḍi, kaṭi⋅paṭṭasūtra⋅ḍorī,
hasta⋅padera jata ābharaṇa
citravarṇa paṭṭaśāḍī, buni photo paṭṭapāḍī,
svarṇa⋅raupya⋅mudrā bahu-dhana

durvā, dhānya, gorocana, haridrā, kuṅkuma, candana,
maṅgala⋅dravya pātra bhariyā
vastra⋅gupta dolā caḍi’, saṅge lañā dāsī ceḍī,
vastrālaṅkāra peṭāri bhariyā

bhakṣya, bhojya, upahāra, saṅge loila bahu bhāra,
śacīgṛhe haila upanīta
dekhiyā bālaka⋅ṭhāma, sākṣāt gokula⋅kāna,
varṇamātra dekhi viparīta

sarva aṅga⋅sunirmāṇa, suvarṇa⋅pratimā⋅bhāna,
sarva aṅga⋅sulakṣaṇamaya
bālakera divya⋅jyoti, dekhi’ pāila bahuprīti,
vātsalyete dravila hṛdaya

durvā, dhānya dila śīrṣe, kaila bahu āśīṣe,
cirajīvi hoo dui bhāi
ḍākinī⋅śā̐khinī haite, śaṅkā upajila cite,
ḍare nāma thuila ‘nimāi’

putramātā⋅snāna⋅dine, dila vastra⋅vibhūṣaṇe,
putra⋅saha miśrere sammāni’
śacī⋅miśrera pūjā lañā, manete hariṣa hañā,
ghare āilā sītā-ṭhākurāṇī

aiche śacī⋅jagannātha, putra pāñā lakṣmī-nātha,
pūrṇa hoila sakala vāñchita
dhana⋅dhānye bhare ghara, lokamānya kalevara,
dine dine haya ānandita

miśra⋅vaiṣṇava, śānta, alampaṭa, śuddha, dānta,
dhanabhoge nāhi abhimāna
putrera prabhāve jata, dhana āsi’ mile tata,
viṣṇu-prīte dvije dena dāna

lagna gaṇi’ harṣamati, nīlāmbara cakravartī,
gupte kichu kahila miśrere
‘mahā-puruṣera cihna, lagne aṅge bhinna bhinna,
dekhi’,⋅ei tāribe saṁsāre’

aiche prabhu śacī-ghare, kṛpāya kaila avatāre,
jei ihā karaye śravaṇa
gaura-prabhu dayā-maya, tā̐’re hayena sadaya,
sei pāya tā̐hāra caraṇa

pāiyā mānuṣa⋅janma, je nā śune gaura-guṇa,
hena janma tā’ra vyartha haila
pāiyā amṛtadhunī, piye viṣagarta⋅pāni,
janmiyā se kene nāhi maila

śrī⋅caitanya⋅nityānanda, ācārya⋅advaitacandra,
svarūpa⋅rūpa⋅raghunāthadāsa
i̐hā sabāra śrī-caraṇa, śire vandi nijadhana,
janmalīlā gāila kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa
en_translation:
From the rising mountain of Nadīyā, the full moon of Lord Gaura⋅hari mercifully arose. The darkness of sin was destroyed, the three worlds rejoiced, and the whole world was filled with the sound of Lord Hari's Name.

At that time, Advaita Ācārya rose up in his own home and danced with a joyful heart. Taking Haridāsa Ṭhākura with him, he danced in the joy of congregational chanting, though no one knew why.

Seeing the eclipse, he smiled and quickly came to the bank of the Ganges to bathe. Taking the eclipse as a pretext, he gave various gifts to the brāhmaṇas with strong conviction.

Seeing the world filled with joy, Haridāsa Ṭhākura hinted: "Seeing your wonderful mood, my mind is very pleased. I see that some great event is about to manifest."

Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita was filled with great joy and went to bathe in the Ganges. His mind overwhelmed with joy, he performed congregational chanting and gave various donations.

In this way, devotees and renunciants wherever they resided received great strength. They danced and performed congregational chanting, their minds overwhelmed with joy, and gave donations on the pretext of the eclipse.

The women of the village, filling plates with various gifts, all came to see. Seeing the form of the child who glowed like raw gold, they gave their blessings, experiencing great happiness.

The celestial women, assuming the guise of village women, also came to see, filling their vessels with various articles.

In the sky, the demigods and celestial musicians danced and sang praises with their instruments. Dancers and musicians from all of Navadvīpa also came and danced with great joy.

Who was coming and who was going, who was dancing and who was singing—no one could keep track. Sorrow and lamentation were dispelled, the people were filled with delight, and Jagannātha Miśra became overwhelmed with joy.

Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita and other devotees came to Jagannātha Miśra and reminded him of his duties. He had the birth ceremony performed according to scriptural injunctions and then gave various donations.

All the gifts he received, and whatever he had in his house, he gave in charity to the priests. He honored all the dancers, singers, and poor people by giving them wealth.

Mālinī-devī, the wife of Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita, along with the wives of other devotees, joyfully offered worship with vermillion, turmeric, oil, and various fruits.

Sītā-Ṭhākurāṇī, the venerable wife of Advaita Ācārya, receiving her husband's order, went with gifts to see the crest-jewel of children.

She brought golden ornaments, silver coins, bangles, divine conch-shells, and various necklaces of gold coins.

She brought tiger-claw pendants set in gold, silk waist-threads, and all manner of ornaments for the hands and feet. She also brought multi-colored silk saris and much wealth in gold and silver coins.

Filling vessels with auspicious articles like grass, turmeric, saffron, and sandalwood, she rode in a covered palanquin with her attendants and baskets full of clothes and ornaments.

She arrived at the house of Mother Śacī with many gifts of food and clothing. Seeing the child's form, she saw directly the Lord of Gokula, only the complexion was different.

All His limbs were perfectly formed, appearing like a golden Deity and endowed with all auspicious marks. Seeing the child's divine effulgence, her heart melted with parental love.

She placed grass and paddy on His head and gave many blessings, saying: "May You two brothers live long." Fearing that witches might cast an evil eye, she named Him "Nimāi."

On the day of the mother and son's first bath, she gave them clothes and ornaments, honoring Jagannātha Miśra as well. Having accepted the hospitality of Mother Śacī and Jagannātha Miśra, Sītā-Ṭhākurāṇī returned home with a joyful heart.

Thus, Mother Śacī and Jagannātha Miśra, having received their Son, had all their desires fulfilled. Their house was filled with wealth and grain, and they became more joyful day by day.

Jagannātha Miśra was a peaceful and pure devotee; he had no pride in his wealth. By the influence of his Son, much wealth came to them, and he gave it all in charity to the priests for the pleasure of the Lord.

Calculating the astrological moment with joy, the grandfather of the Lord secretly told Jagannātha Miśra: "I see the marks of a great personality upon His limbs; He will surely deliver the entire world."

Thus the Lord mercifully made His advent in Mother Śacī's house. Whoever hears this narrative will receive the mercy of Lord Gaura and eventually attain His lotus feet.

Having attained a human birth, one who does not hear the glories of Lord Gaura makes such a birth useless. Having found a river of nectar, if one chooses to drink from a poisonous pit, why did they not simply die after taking birth?

I worship the lotus feet of Śrī Caitanya, Nityānanda, Advaita Ācārya, Svarūpa Dāmodara, Rūpa Gosvāmī, and Raghunātha Dāsa Gosvāmī. Kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa Kavirāja has thus sung the pastimes of the Lord's birth.
